intent: We are not building the Forge Agent architecture. We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS two-process AI coding agent (Swift shell + Python backend) that uses dual-model consensus AI (Claude + GPT-4o with Claude arbitrating) to autonomously decompose build intents into ordered PRDs and PRs, generate implementation and test code via parallel consensus generation, validate through a multi-stage quality pipeline (self-correction → lint gate → 20-pass fix loop → CI gate), and open GitHub pull requests gated on operator approval. The system encompasses 16 TRD-specified subsystems: macOS Application Shell (Swift/SwiftUI with Touch ID, Keychain, XPC), Consensus Engine, Build Pipeline with confidence-gated scope, Multi-Agent Coordination via GitHub-stored Build Ledger, GitHub Integration Layer, Holistic Code Review (5-lens), TRD Development Workflow (FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes), UI/UX Design System, Mac CI Runner, Document Store (FAISS + sentence-transformers), Security Threat Model enforcement, Backend Runtime Startup/Handshake, Recovery and State Management (3-layer persistence, per-PR checkpoints, BuildMemory, BuildRules), Code Quality CI Pipeline, Operational Runbook tooling, and Agent Testing/Validation (FM taxonomy, 35 regression tests).

Clarifications:
  - Same product and Crafted is the product
  - refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs
  - refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices
  - refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices. Provide later in the build cycle
  - refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices
  - refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices
  - refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices
  - refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices
  - refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices
  - Crafted is the product name

Operator on 'This is a full-platform build spanning 16 TRDs, Swift + Pyth':
single build target

Operator on 'The TRDs reference an existing codebase at version 38.153.0 ':
refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices

Operator on 'TRD-8 §24 introduces Figma MCP Server integration and sketch':
refer to the trds but resolve any ambiguities in the PRDs and use best practices

Operator on 'The branch naming convention uses 'forge-agent/build/' prefi':
migrate to crafted/build/

prd_plan.title: Foundation Architecture, Versioning, and Repository Alignment
macOS Swift Shell, App Lifecycle, and Native Runtime Container
Authentication, Keychain, Touch ID, and Session Security
XPC Bridge, Handshake Protocol, and Backend Process Management
Backend Runtime Startup, Compatibility Handshake, and Degraded Operation
Consensus Engine, Dual-Model Generation, and Arbitration
Document Store, Retrieval, Injection Defense, and Build Context Assembly
GitHub Integration Layer, Branch/Commit/PR Lifecycle, and CI Polling
Scope Confidence Gate, PRD Planning, and Ordered Build Decomposition
PR Planning, PR Type Routing, and Execution Contracts
Per-PR Generation Pipeline, Self-Correction, Lint Gate, and Fix Loop
Recovery, Thread State, BuildMemory, BuildRules, and Resume Semantics
Multi-Agent Coordination and GitHub Build Ledger
Code Quality CI Pipeline, Workflow Generation, and Mac Runner Integration
Holistic Code Review Workflow and Five-Lens Review Automation
TRD Development Workflow and Multi-Mode Specification Authoring
Security Threat Model Enforcement and Safe Failure Controls
UI/UX Design System, Design-to-Code Inputs, and Figma Pipeline
Operational Runbook Tooling, Diagnostics, and Operator Controls
Agent Testing, Regression Contract, and Validation Harness

1.title: Establish repository directory structure and foundational scaffolding
1.summary: Create the canonical directory layout for the Crafted project: `forge-standards/`, `docs/`, `src/`, `swift-shell/`, `tests/`, `.github/workflows/`, and `scripts/`. Add placeholder `__init__.py` files, `.gitkeep` in empty directories, and base `.editorconfig` / `.gitignore` files. This is the skeleton every subsequent PR writes into.

2.title: Repository audit and TRD ambiguity inventory
2.summary: Creates a non-runtime audit of repository identity, version touchpoints, branch-prefix references, environment variable prefixes, and cross-TRD ambiguity hotspots. Produces `forge-standards/REPO_AUDIT.md` and `forge-standards/TRD_AMBIGUITIES.md` to anchor all subsequent foundation decisions.

3.title: Define canonical decisions and cross-TRD interface contracts
3.summary: Create `forge-standards/DECISIONS.md` — an Architecture Decision Record (ADR) log resolving all ambiguities identified across the 16 TRDs. Each decision includes: ID, TRD references, ambiguity statement, resolution, and rationale. Create `forge-standards/INTERFACES.md` defining every cross-process and cross-subsystem interface contract: XPC message schema, Python↔Swift handshake protocol, consensus engine API surface, build pipeline stage contracts, GitHub integration API, and document store query interface. Uses the audit from PR-2 as input and resolves conflicts using best practices.

4.title: Naming convention reference and project glossary
4.summary: Create `forge-standards/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md` codifying all symbol naming rules: Python module names (`snake_case`), Swift type names (`PascalCase`), environment variables (`CRAFTED_UPPER_SNAKE`), branch prefixes (`crafted/build/`), PR title format, and TRD/PRD ID formats. Create `forge-standards/GLOSSARY.md` defining every domain term used across TRDs (e.g., Build Ledger, Consensus Engine, Build Intent, PRD, Quality Pipeline, Confidence Gate). These are the canonical references for all future implementation.

5.title: TRD implementation dependency graph and late-build integration sequence
5.summary: Create `forge-standards/BUILD_SEQUENCE.md` containing: (1) a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of all 16 TRDs showing implementation dependencies, (2) the ordered build phases with which TRDs belong to each phase, (3) parallelization opportunities within each phase, (4) integration gates where cross-subsystem testing must pass before the next phase begins, (5) late-build items explicitly deferred (Figma MCP, advanced Document Store tuning, FM taxonomy full coverage). Include a Mermaid diagram of the DAG. Reference DECISIONS.md for any sequencing ambiguity resolutions.

6.title: Policy tests for foundation standards documents
6.summary: Adds tests that validate required sections and key canonical values in the foundation standards documentation: DECISIONS.md has minimum required decision entries, INTERFACES.md covers all cross-process contracts, BUILD_SEQUENCE.md contains a valid Mermaid DAG, NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md and GLOSSARY.md exist and are non-empty. Ensures downstream work can rely on stable decisions and interface references.

7.title: Align repository product identity to Crafted across root documentation
7.summary: Rewrite `README.md` so all headings, descriptions, architecture diagram references, TRD index table, and command references use 'Crafted' (not 'Forge Agent'). Update `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` narrative text to 'Crafted'. Verify all environment variable references use the `CRAFTED_` prefix. Add `docs/NAMING_MIGRATION.md` listing every renamed symbol and its old→new mapping for downstream contributor reference.

8.title: Define version contract: VERSION file, pyproject.toml, and sync tooling
8.summary: Create the single source-of-truth `VERSION` file at the repo root (initial value `0.1.0`). Create `pyproject.toml` with `[project].name = 'crafted-dev-agent'` and `version` dynamically read from `VERSION`. Add `scripts/sync_version.py` — a CLI tool that reads `VERSION` and patches both `pyproject.toml` and `Info.plist` to match, exiting non-zero on drift. Document the semver compatibility rules in `forge-standards/VERSION_CONTRACT.md`.

9.title: Swift shell version and bundle metadata contract with Info.plist stub
9.summary: Create `swift-shell/Crafted/Info.plist` stub with `CFBundleShortVersionString` and `CFBundleVersion` set to `0.1.0`. Document the required Info.plist keys, CFBundle version mapping, and synchronization rules in `forge-standards/SWIFT_VERSION_CONTRACT.md` so future Swift implementation work conforms to the repository-wide version source of truth. Update `scripts/sync_version.py` to patch Info.plist.

10.title: Tests for version contract synchronization
10.summary: Implement version consistency tests: verify `VERSION`, `pyproject.toml`, and `Info.plist` all report the same semver string. Test `scripts/sync_version.py` for correct patching, idempotency, and non-zero exit on intentional drift. Tests run as pure pytest with no runtime dependencies.

11.title: Align startup and configuration code to canonical CRAFTED_ environment variable prefixes
11.summary: Audit and update `src/config.py` and `src/agent.py` for any legacy `FORGE_` prefixed environment variables. Create `src/config.py` with a canonical `CraftedConfig` dataclass that reads all environment variables with `CRAFTED_` prefix, provides defaults, and validates required values. Add a `CRAFTED_ENV_PREFIX = 'CRAFTED_'` constant. Document every environment variable in `forge-standards/ENV_VARS.md` with name, type, default, and which TRD specifies it.

12.title: Tests for environment variable prefix compliance and CraftedConfig
12.summary: Add tests that grep all `.py`, `.swift`, `.md`, and `.yaml` files for any remaining `FORGE_` prefixed env vars (must find zero outside documented migration contexts). Unit test `CraftedConfig` dataclass for default values, required-field validation errors, and correct `CRAFTED_` prefix reading from mock environment.

13.title: Branch convention migration plan: forge-agent/build/* to crafted/build/*
13.summary: Create `forge-standards/BRANCH_MIGRATION.md` documenting the transition plan from `forge-agent/build/*` to `crafted/build/*` branch prefixes. Define: (1) cutover date/trigger, (2) dual-prefix support window where CI accepts both patterns, (3) GitHub Actions workflow updates needed, (4) branch protection rule changes. Update `.github/workflows/ci.yml` CI trigger patterns to accept both `forge-agent/build/**` and `crafted/build/**` during the transition. Add a `scripts/migrate_branch_prefix.sh` helper that renames local branches and updates remote tracking.

14.title: Tests for branch naming convention compliance
14.summary: Add tests that validate: (1) the current branch name matches `crafted/build/*` or `main` or is within the documented transition window accepting `forge-agent/build/*`, (2) `scripts/migrate_branch_prefix.sh` correctly renames a mock branch, (3) `.github/workflows/ci.yml` trigger patterns include both prefixes. Implemented as pytest tests that parse YAML and shell scripts.

15.title: CI workflow for PRD-001 foundational checks
15.summary: Create `.github/workflows/foundation-checks.yml` — a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on every push to `crafted/build/**` and `main`. It executes: (1) version consistency tests from PR-10, (2) env prefix compliance tests from PR-12, (3) branch convention tests from PR-14, (4) foundation doc policy tests from PR-6, (5) a doc-lint step that verifies all `forge-standards/*.md` files are valid Markdown with no broken internal links. This is the CI gate that enforces PRD-001 invariants for all future PRDs.

16.title: Repository identity and naming compliance policy tests
16.summary: Add tests that scan repository docs and configuration for canonical Crafted naming: (1) `README.md` contains 'Crafted' and not 'Forge Agent' in headings, (2) `pyproject.toml` name is `crafted-dev-agent`, (3) no file in the repo contains `FORGE_` env var prefixes outside of documented migration contexts. Prevents regression during later subsystem development.

17.title: End-to-end PRD-001 compliance test suite and acceptance checklist
17.summary: A comprehensive integration test that validates the complete PRD-001 deliverable: (1) all `forge-standards/` decision documents exist and are non-empty, (2) version contract is enforced across all touchpoints, (3) naming conventions and glossary are complete, (4) branch migration is documented and CI-enforced, (5) environment variable alignment is complete, (6) build sequence DAG is valid. Also publishes `forge-standards/PRD-001_ACCEPTANCE.md` as the final acceptance checklist tying together all foundation work so downstream PRDs can begin against an explicit baseline.
